metadata.title: Base mobile review rubric
metadata.description: Base rubric for mobile-platform review (iOS/Android). Layered atop the universal base rubrics (code/perf/security/a11y) by /gaia-review-mobile. Sub-rubrics (mobile-code, mobile-perf, mobile-security, mobile-a11y) carry skill-specific deltas; this base captures cross-cutting mobile concerns: UI/UX conventions, lifecycle, offline/connectivity, resource management.

severity_rules.pattern: Custom UI controls deviate from platform conventions (e.g., iOS HIG navigation patterns, Material Design components on Android) without justification.
severity_rules.description: Platform UI conventions reduce learnability. Custom controls that ignore HIG/Material guidelines force users to relearn navigation and increase support load.
severity_rules.remediation: Use platform-native controls (UINavigationController, BottomNavigationView) where possible; document deliberate deviations in a UX design note.

severity_rules.pattern: App ignores safe-area insets on iOS (notch, home indicator) or system bars on Android (status bar, navigation bar).
severity_rules.description: Content rendered behind system chrome is unreadable or untappable on modern devices. Safe-area handling is mandatory for App Store / Play Store approval.

severity_rules.pattern: iOS app uses Android-style toast notifications or Android app uses iOS-style modal sheets without platform adaptation.
severity_rules.description: Cross-platform UI patterns that ignore the host platform's idioms feel foreign and damage user trust.
severity_rules.remediation: Use UIAlertController / SnackBar appropriate to the platform; abstract notification UI behind a platform-aware component.

severity_rules.pattern: App uses non-standard back-navigation behaviour: iOS swipe-back disabled without alternative, or Android hardware-back ignored / mishandled.
severity_rules.description: Breaking back-navigation contracts is one of the top complaints in app-store reviews. iOS users expect swipe-from-edge; Android users expect hardware back to navigate up.
severity_rules.remediation: Re-enable interactivePopGestureRecognizer; on Android, implement onBackPressed / OnBackPressedDispatcher to consume back navigation correctly.

severity_rules.pattern: App fails to persist transient UI state (form input, scroll position, selection) across backgrounding or process death.
severity_rules.description: Mobile OSes aggressively reclaim memory; an app may be killed while backgrounded and relaunched into a stale state. State restoration is part of the platform contract.
severity_rules.remediation: Use NSUserActivity + state restoration on iOS; SavedStateHandle + onSaveInstanceState on Android. Persist work-in-progress to disk on willResignActive / onPause.

severity_rules.pattern: Long-running task (download, upload, computation) runs only on the foreground without a background-task contract.
severity_rules.description: Foreground-only tasks are interrupted when the user backgrounds the app, leaving partial state. Background-task APIs exist precisely for this case.
severity_rules.remediation: Use BGTaskScheduler / URLSession background config (iOS) or WorkManager / ForegroundService (Android) with the appropriate notification.

severity_rules.pattern: App assumes a network connection is available; fails or shows a generic error when offline instead of falling back to cached data.
severity_rules.description: Mobile users frequently transition between online and offline (subway, elevator, airplane). Offline-unaware apps are unreliable in real-world use.
severity_rules.remediation: Cache critical data locally; show last-known data with a 'stale' indicator; queue writes via WorkManager / NSURLSession background queue.

severity_rules.pattern: Network requests do not retry on transient failures (timeouts, DNS resolution, captive portals) or use unbounded retry without backoff.
severity_rules.description: Mobile networks are lossy. Naive retry or no retry both fail users — bounded exponential backoff is the standard pattern.
severity_rules.remediation: Use exponential-backoff-with-jitter (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s, max 30s); cap retries; surface a user-facing 'try again' affordance after the cap.

severity_rules.pattern: App holds GPS, camera, microphone, or Bluetooth sessions open while in the background or after the user navigates away from the consuming screen.
severity_rules.description: Idle hardware sessions drain battery and trigger OS warnings to the user (orange/green dot, battery-usage screens). Both stores penalise apps with high background-resource usage.
severity_rules.remediation: Release the resource in viewDidDisappear / onPause; re-acquire on viewWillAppear / onResume; use the platform's lifecycle-aware APIs.

severity_rules.pattern: App allocates large bitmaps, video buffers, or audio assets without bounding by available memory or releasing on memory-warning notifications.
severity_rules.description: Mobile devices have hard memory ceilings (~150-300 MB for many iOS app classes). OOM kills are silent and look like crashes to users.
severity_rules.remediation: Subscribe to memory-warning notifications; downsample bitmaps to display size; reuse buffers; profile with Instruments / Android Studio Profiler.
